Short cash flows discounted using an overnight sovereign curve and FX hedging in the low band both point to the same sub-column within that group (the cell with "overnight sovereign curve reference for discounting short cashflows" and "low effectiveness band under 70% due to basis mismatch"). Step 2 — Identify deferred tax positioning under regional shock: Under "regional market liquidity shock due to geopolitical event" in that same sub-column (Table 0) the deferred tax positioning is "valuation allowance applied to uncertain tax position deferred assets." Step 3 — Use fiscal cadence and cash-sweep hierarchy: Fiscal year ending April 30 with regulatory overlay corresponds to the three-column Fiscal-April-30 group in Table 1. The cash sweep "second after mandatory tax and operating costs" matches the first column of that group (the second-tier sweep entry). For the deferred-tax row "valuation allowance applied to uncertain tax position deferred assets" in that Fiscal-April-30 / second-tier-sweep column (Table 1), the servicing/credit-enhancement entry is "servicing escrow partially funded with scheduled replenishment (true sale securitisation credit wrap with excess spread support)". Therefore the credit enhancement method is "true sale securitisation credit wrap with excess spread support." Step 4 — Select relevant values and determine maximum adjusted enterprise value: In Table 2, identify the numeric enterprise-value cells that correspond to capital tiers where the form of credit enhancement is the "true sale securitisation credit wrap with excess spread support" and under the tax-loss utilisation schedule "scheduled utilisation of tax losses across forecast horizon subject to caps" (the scheduled-utilisation tax-loss column). Scanning those relevant cells across the capital tiers and liquidity-buffer classifications yields the maximum numeric value 118462000306.920000 GHS. Final answer: 118462000306.920000
